

Drinking water levels linked to suicide rates

>In line with the majority of findings from other countries, our results indicate that the intake of lithium with drinking water may have a gender-dependent suicide-protective effect.
>https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34800573/

Linked to lower dementia levels

>Association between trace-Li levels and a lower risk or mortality from dementia were observed at concentrations of Li in drinking water as low as 0.002 mg/L and 0.056 mg/L.
>https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39212809/

Low doses might work

>Clinical and molecular effects of lithium are numerous, and its effects also appear to have a certain degree of specificity related to the dose administered. In sum, the clinical effects of lithium are maximal for mood stabilisation at concentrations higher than 0.6 mEq/l. However, lower levels may be sufficient for preventing depressive recurrences in older populations of patients, and microdoses could be effective in decreasing suicide risk, especially in patients with BD.
>https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38914810/


# Dose

5mg shows up in the brain, and is much lower than therapeutic doses of 150-300mg

>Lithium [orotate](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/orotic-acid) supplements were taken by nine healthy adult male subjects (5 mg daily) for up to 28 days, providing 2–7 % of the lithium content of a typical therapeutic [lithium carbonate](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/pharmacology-toxicology-and-pharmaceutical-science/lithium-carbonate) dose. One-dimensional 7Li-images were acquired on a 3.0 T MRI scanner. All subjects were scanned on day 14 or 28; seven were scanned on both, one at baseline and one after 7-days washout.
>https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0165032724008723

### Results

7Li-MR signal amplitude was broadly stable between days 14 and 28. Two subjects had notably higher 7Li-signal intensities (approximately 2–4×) compared to other study participants.

### Limitations

Lithium adherence was self-reported by all participants without formal validation. The coarse spatial resolution necessary for detection of low concentrations of 7Li exhibits imperfect spatial separation of signal from adjacent pixels.

### Conclusions

7Li-MRI performed using a clinical 3T scanner demonstrated detection of lithium in the brain at very low concentration, in the range of approximately 10–60 mM.


- Not enough good quality studies or dosing amounts isn't clear so don't recommend yet.

[Low-Dose of This Mineral REVERSES Dementia?](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h0b3EfuKaYQ)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Lithium linked to lower levels of dementia

Lithium orotate is the type that works - but limited studies and don't know optimal dosing

We get lithium from diet and water supply so recommends not taking it yet,
